compiled_state.title: As a DSO plugin user, conversations include sub-agent delegation guidance via the startup hook
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Add a Sub-Agent Delegation section to the using-dso skill with a Red Flags rationalization table covering three delegation patterns, and update the skill eval to validate the new content.
**Why**: Claude defaults to doing all work inline — reading files sequentially, editing serially, self-reviewing its output. Startup-hook guidance in the Red Flags pattern (proven effective for skill invocation) will shape behavior toward sub-agent delegation where appropriate.
**Scope**:
- IN: Author Sub-Agent Delegation section in SKILL.md with Red Flags table (Thought/Reality columns) covering: sequential multi-file reads (10+), serial independent file edits, inline self-review. Update HOOK-INJECTION.md with matching injection content. Update or create skill eval config for using-dso to validate Red Flags table is present and well-formed. Ensure guidance includes scope limitation note to prevent recursive agent spawning.
- OUT: Naming specific reviewer agents (defer to dso-8l5h), sub-agent cap mechanics (defer to dso-t7af), tool allowlists (defer to dso-9fgn), task decomposition mechanics (defer to 2e8e-72d9), token budgeting (defer to dso-zj3r)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, the using-dso SKILL.md contains a Sub-Agent Delegation section with a Red Flags table using Thought and Reality columns
  ← Satisfies: "The sub-agent guidance follows the Red Flags table pattern"
- When this story is complete, the Red Flags table identifies three patterns: sequential multi-file reads (10+ files), serial independent file edits, and inline self-review
  ← Satisfies: SC1, SC2, SC3 — "identifies sequential multi-file reads as a Red Flag", "identifies serial independent file edits as a Red Flag", "identifies inline self-review as a Red Flag"
- When this story is complete, the sub-agent guidance section does not exceed 60 rendered lines in the skill file inclusive of blank lines and headers
  ← Satisfies: "The sub-agent guidance section does not exceed 60 rendered lines"
- When this story is complete, the HOOK-INJECTION.md file includes the sub-agent delegation guidance for injection into conversations
  ← Satisfies: SC1-3 (guidance must be injected to have effect)
- When this story is complete, the skill eval suite validates that the sub-agent Red Flags table content is present and well-formed
  ← Satisfies: "The skill eval suite validates that the sub-agent Red Flags table content is present and well-formed"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Scenario analysis flagged recursive agent spawning risk — guidance must include explicit scope note that sub-agents should not re-apply startup hook delegation guidance to spawn further sub-agents
- [Maintainability] Guidance must not conflict with CLAUDE.md Never-Do rules (rule 4: max 5 sub-agents at a time, rule 5: commit before new batch) — note compatibility or cross-reference
- [Completeness] Both SKILL.md and HOOK-INJECTION.md contain YAML frontmatter with name: using-lockpick. After Story 1 renames the directory, ensure the frontmatter name field reads using-dso in both files. The frontmatter name is used by Claude Code skill auto-discovery.
